summary:
Zinc polycarboxylate cement has an extremely short working time due to its fast setting nature through chemical reaction after mixing; it must be used immediately without delay. The specific duration may vary slightly based on environmental factors yet remains very brief compared to many dental restorative products. To summarize concisely:

Zinc polycarboxylate cement sets rapidly and should be applied right away after mixing because its usable period lasts only seconds or minutes at most under normal circumstances. Key point highlighted involves immediacy required during placement procedures despite slight variability noted across studies regarding precise timing limits imposed by this material’s chemistry. Agent1 concludes focusing strictly on critical aspects related directly answering query posed while omitting less pertinent details not essential for understanding core functionality concerning usage constraints associated with said type of adhesive compound utilized within dentistry practice settings today.
